case_brief: Aisha Williams, a 34-year-old Black female attorney at Greystone Law Partners, was denied partnership and subsequently constructively discharged after the firm made her working conditions intolerable. Williams was the only Black attorney at the firm. After she was passed over for partnership (the position was given to a white male associate with fewer billable hours and lower client ratings), the firm reduced her client assignments by 70%, moved her to an office with no windows, excluded her from senior partner meetings she had previously attended, and assigned her work below her seniority level. Williams complained internally about disparate treatment in partnership decisions. Within six weeks of that complaint, three additional adverse actions were taken against her. Williams formally resigned after eight months of this treatment. She had an offer letter that described her as a 'partnership-track associate' and included specific partnership evaluation criteria she exceeded. Jurisdiction: 4th Circuit (Virginia).
